match_fields.target_node: Baylor College of Medicine Cardiovascular Surgery

upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 26635916 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2015 | content_match] Winters WL. 'Houston Hearts: A History of Cardiovascular Surgery and Medicine.' Documents DeBakey's arrival at Baylor in 1948, recruitment of Cooley (1951), Crawford (1954), and creation of the cardiovascular surgery program.

[Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 26635916 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2015 | content_match] States that 'Edward B. Dietrich and George P. Noon finished their cardiovascular surgical training and joined DeBakey' in 1965.

[Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 26635916 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2015 | content_match] Winters article names DeBakey's team including Mattox. BCM History of Leadership page confirms Mattox trained under DeBakey. Mattox also authored PMID 20073164 (Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2009) as a DeBakey tribute.

[Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 26635916 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2015 | content_match] Names Morris among DeBakey's legendary surgical associates. BCM History of Leadership confirms Morris as DeBakey trainee.

upgrades.edge_index_hint: DeBakey -> H. Edward Garrett Sr. (direct_training, c.1958-1964)
upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 26635916 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2015 | content_match] States 'Dr. Ed Garrett completed his cardiovascular surgical training at Baylor and joined the DeBakey staff' and 'in 1964 performed the first aorta-to-coronary artery bypass.' BCM History of Leadership confirms training under DeBakey.

[Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 26635916 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Methodist Debakey Cardiovasc J 2015 | content_match] Names 'Oscar Creech Jr. (1949)' as one of DeBakey's first associates at Baylor. BCM History of Leadership confirms Creech joined DeBakey in 1949.

no_upgrade_available.edge: Edward Churchill -> MGH Thoracic Surgical Service (institutional_founder, 1931-1962)
no_upgrade_available.reason: Churchill's MGH thoracic service documented in institutional histories. PMID 20134311 (already used for Med Theater edge) covers his wartime role, not the MGH founding.

The Taussig-Blalock blue baby collaboration is one of the most documented events in surgical history, but the existing graph edge is typed as observational_study (Taussig approaching Blalock with the concept). Multiple PMIDs exist for the Blalock-Taussig shunt itself but verifying which specifically documents the Taussig→Blalock conceptual transfer would require full-text review. Mark for future deep-dive.
